old_claim.note: Fusion Rockets is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: Rocketry provides the engine, propellant-flow, thrust-generation, and spacecraft propulsion lineage for antimatter annihilation rocket concepts.

source_shape.source_locator: The NASA contractor report describes antimatter annihilation for spacecraft propulsion and identifies the rocket engine as the component where annihilation occurs and thrust is produced.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Antimatter rockets are rocket propulsion systems using matter-antimatter annihilation rather than a subtype that necessarily depends on fusion rockets.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fusion appears in some hybrid antimatter-catalyzed concepts, but the broader node is defined by rocket propulsion and antimatter annihilation, so rocketry is the direct lineage and fusion rockets are not a prerequisite.

ontology_before: The graph made antimatter rockets depend directly on fusion rockets, implying antimatter annihilation rockets require prior fusion rocket propulsion rather than treating fusion as one possible hybrid design.
ontology_after: The graph routes antimatter rockets through general rocketry plus antimatter production/containment and advanced materials, preserving fusion rockets as a separate advanced propulsion family.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: antimatter_rockets now depends on rocketry and no longer directly on fusion_rockets.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped only to antimatter-catalyzed fusion propulsion rather than antimatter annihilation rockets broadly.
A source shows all antimatter rocket concepts require a prior fusion rocket architecture rather than rocket propulsion and antimatter handling.
A dedicated antimatter-catalyzed microfusion node is added and should carry the fusion_rockets edge separately.
short_reason: Fusion rockets are an adjacent or hybrid propulsion path, while rocketry is the direct propulsion lineage for antimatter annihilation rockets.
